revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:藤田記念庭園]]
'''Fujita Memorial Garden''' (藤田記念庭園) is a {{ill|Japanese garden|en|Japanese garden|qid=Q15835|lt=Japanese garden|1=Japanese garden}} located in {{ill|Hirosaki|en|Hirosaki|qid=Q194087|lt=Hirosaki|1=Hirosaki}}, {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|en|Aomori Prefecture|qid=Q71699|lt=Aomori Prefecture|1=Aomori Prefecture}}. The garden includes a Western-style house, a Japanese-style house, and a warehouse, which were registered as {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11579194}} in {{ill|2003|en|2003|qid=Q1986|lt=2003|1=2003}}.
== History == The garden was created in {{ill|1919|en|1919|qid=Q2157|lt=1919|1=1919}} ({{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|qid=Q329174|lt=Taisho 8|1=Taishō era}}) by {{ill|Ken'ichi Fujita|ja|藤田謙一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken'ichi Fujita|1=Ken'ichi Fujita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11624901}}, a businessman who served as the chairman of the {{ill|Japan Chamber of Commerce and Industry|ja|日本商工会議所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Chamber of Commerce and Industry|1=The Japan Chamber of Commerce and Industry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11507549}}. Fujita invited a landscape gardener from Tokyo to design the garden in the Edo style for his private residence in his hometown, Hirosaki.
The Japanese-style house, originally constructed in {{ill|1937|en|1937|qid=Q18647|lt=1937|1=1937}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Showa 12|1=Shōwa era}}) in {{ill|Itayanagi, Aomori|en|Itayanagi, Aomori|qid=Q1347574|lt=Itayanagi|1=Itayanagi, Aomori}} as Fujita's main residence, was relocated to its current location in {{ill|1961|en|1961|qid=Q3696|lt=1961|1=1961}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Showa 36|1=Shōwa era}}).
After Fujita's death, the property was transferred to {{ill|Toshiyo Karakushi|ja|唐牛敏世|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshiyo Karakushi|1=Binzei Karouji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11418663}}, the president of {{ill|Hirosaki Mutual Bank|ja|弘前相互銀行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirosaki Mutual Bank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to みちのく銀行|1=Michinoku Bank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6837907}} (later {{ill|Michinoku Bank|ja|みちのく銀行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoku Bank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Michinoku Bank|qid=Q6837907}}), and was opened to the public as the "Hirosaki Mutual Bank Club" (later "Michinoku Bank Club"). Following Karakushi's death in {{ill|1979|en|1979|qid=Q2484|lt=1979|1=1979}}, the property was neglected until {{ill|1987|en|1987|qid=Q2429|lt=1987|1=1987}}, when {{ill|Hirosaki|en|Hirosaki|qid=Q194087|lt=Hirosaki City|1=Hirosaki}} purchased it as part of its 100th-anniversary celebrations. The restored garden opened to the public in {{ill|1991|en|1991|qid=Q2062|lt=1991|1=1991}} ({{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|qid=Q234510|lt=Heisei 3|1=Heisei era}}).
== Overview == * Total area: 21,800 m<sup>2</sup> * Style: A combination of a Western-style garden and a {{ill|strolling pond garden|ja|池泉回遊式庭園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/strolling pond garden|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 回遊式庭園|1=Strolling garden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11420231}} * Completed: 1919 (Taisho 8) * Location: 8-1 Kamishirogane-cho, Hirosaki City, Aomori Prefecture 036-8207 * Cultural designation: Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan (registered in July 2003: Western-style house, Japanese-style house, warehouse, Kabuki gate, and side guardhouses)
The garden is divided into two parts by a 13-meter-high escarpment. The u11er section features a Western-style garden with a view of {{ill|Mount Iwaki|en|Mount Iwaki|qid=Q713857|lt=Mt. Iwaki|1=Mount Iwaki}}, while the lower section is a Japanese-style strolling pond garden. It is one of the largest gardens in the Tohoku region, spanning approximately 21,800 square meters.
The Western-style house, located on the u11er section, is a two-story wooden and mortar structure featuring an eye-catching eight-sided tower and a distinctive hakama-skirt roof. It was designed by Horie Kanzo, the sixth son of {{ill|Sakichi Horie|ja|堀江佐吉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakichi Horie|1=Horie Sakichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11427510}}, and constructed by his eldest son, Hiko Saburo. The warehouse, built for an agricultural development project near the foot of Mt. Iwaki, is a two-story brick building with English bond brickwork and quadruple-layered eaves.
Visitors can explore the buildings, including the Western-style house, which houses the "Taisho Roman Cafe," a 50-person hall, and a small meeting room. The warehouse (archaeological museum) exhibits artifacts excavated from sites such as the {{ill|Sunazawa site|ja|砂沢遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunazawa site|1=Sunazawa Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11587457}}, a nationally designated Important Cultural Property, and other local archaeological findings.
<gallery> Japanese style house of the Fujita Memorial Garden 20220618a.jpg|Japanese-style house Tea ceremony house of the Fujita Memorial Garden.jpg|Tea house Western style house of the Fujita Memorial Garden 20220618b.jpg|Western-style house Cafe in the western-style house of the Fujita Memorial Garden.jpg|Interior of the cafe </gallery>
== Access == * {{ill|Ōu Main Line|en|Ōu Main Line|qid=Q386495|lt=Ou Main Line|1=Ōu Main Line}} {{ill|Hirosaki Station|en|Hirosaki Station|qid=Q5770722|lt=Hirosaki Station|1=Hirosaki Station}}, 20 minutes by bus, get off at "City Hall Park Entrance," then a 3-minute walk. * {{ill|Tōhoku Expressway|en|Tōhoku Expressway|qid=Q428428|lt=Tohoku Expressway|1=Tōhoku Expressway}} {{ill|Owani Hirosaki Interchange|ja|大鰐弘前インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owani Hirosaki Interchange|1=Owani-Hirosaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10938610}}, 30 minutes by car.
== See Also == * {{ill|Flying Witch|en|Flying Witch|qid=Q20926303|lt=Flying Witch|1=Flying Witch}} – A manga by {{ill|Chihiro Ishizuka|ja|石塚千尋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chihiro Ishizuka|1=Chihiro Ishizuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q24666112}}. The cafe "Conclusio" featured in the manga is modeled after the Western-style house in the garden.
== External Links == {{commonscat}} * {{official website}} * [http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/culture/torokuyukei/17.html Fujita Family Former Residence - Western-style house (Aomori Prefecture Cultural Assets)] * [http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/culture/torokuyukei/18.html Fujita Family Former Residence - Japanese-style house (Aomori Prefecture Cultural Assets)] * [http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/culture/torokuyukei/19.html Fujita Family Former Residence - Warehouse (Aomori Prefecture Cultural Assets)] * [http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/culture/torokuyukei/20.html Fujita Family Former Residence - Kabuki Gate and Side Guardhouses (Aomori Prefecture Cultural Assets)] {{translated page|ja|藤田記念庭園|version=103274813|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:藤田記念庭園]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Fujita Memorial Garden}} {{wikidata link|Q11624898}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hirosaki, Aomori]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Japan completed in 1919]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Japan completed in 1937]] [[Category:Japanese Gardens in the Tōhoku Region]] [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Parks and gardens in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan in Aomori prefecture]] [[Category:Western Style Houses]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
